THE VESSEL TO HAVE THE LIBERTY OF USING LSMGO FOR EXCHANGING BALLAST WATER AT SEA,STARTING/STANDBY/STOPPING ENGINES, WHILE MANEUVERING IN SHALLOW/NARROW WATERS, CANALS, RIVERS, IN/OUT OF PORTS AND DURING POOR VISIBILITY AND ALSO DURING MAINTENANCE WORKS, NARROW/CONFINED WATERS ETC. THE VESSEL WILL CONSUME ADDITIONAL VLSFO WHEN BALLASTING/DEBALLASTING/BALLAST EXCHANGING/HOLD CLEANNING ETC.
ALL ABOVE SPEED/CONSUMPTIONS ARE GIVEN UNDER GOOD WEATHER CONDITION AND CLEAN BOTTOM, SMOOTH SEA, WITH BEAUFORT SCALE MAX WIND FORCE 4 AND DOUGLAS SEA STATE 3 (THREE, I.E. THE SUM OF WAVE + SWELL HEIGHT UP TO 1.25 METERS),NO ADVERSE CURRENT AND NO NEGATIVE INFLUENCE OF SWELL. EXTRAPOLATION OF
“
GOOD WEATHER
”
PERFORMANCE FOR
“
BAD WEATHER
”
PERIODS IS NOT ALLOWED. POSITIVE CURRENT FACTOR ON SHIP\'S FAVOR.

LSFO: IF380CST-RMG380-LS ISO8217-2017, SULPHUR CONTENT NOT MORE THAN 0.50%

LSMGO: MGO-DMA-LS ISO8217-2017, SULPHUR CONTENT NOT MORE THAN 0.10%

THE MASTER ALWAYS HAS AN OPTION TO RUN AT FULL SPEED FOR ABOUT 2 HOURS ON DAILY BASIS,
FOR ECONOMIC REASONS CHARTERERS HAVE THE OPTION TO ORDER THE VESSEL TO PROCEED AT INCREASED OR REDUCED SPEED AT ANY TIME AND FOR ANY PERIOD OF TIME DURING THIS CHARTER PARTY. IF MASTER/CHIEF ENGINEERS FIND SUCH SPEED ADJUSTMENT WILL DAMAGE THE M/E, THE MASTER/CHIEF ENGINEER HAVE THEIR OWN DECISION TO RESUME SAILING AT CP SPEED/CONSUMPTION AT ANY TIME DURING THE PERIOD OF THIS C/P.

SPEED& CONSUMPTION BASIS SCANTLING DRAFT:
- BALLAST ABOUT 12.62 KNOTS ON ABOUT 22.5MT LSFO AND 0.1 MT LSMGO
- LADEN ABOUT 12.50 KNOTS ON ABOUT 23.5MT LSFO AND 0.1 MT LSMGO
- PORT IDLE ABOUT 3.5MT LSFO/WORKING ABOUT 5.0MT LSFO
ECO SPEED FOR REFERENCE ONLY WITHOUT GUARANTEE:
-ABT 12.0KTS (BALLAST) ON ABT 21.5MT LSFO 380CST+ABT 0.1MTS LSMGO
-ABT 12.0KTS (LADEN) ON ABT 22.5MT LSFO 380CST+ABT 0.1MTS LSMGO
(ALL ABOUT MEANS AN ALLOWANCE OF 0.5 KNOT ON SPEED AND 5% ON CONSUMPTION)
BUNKER QUALITY
ISO F-8217:2017 RMG380 FOR VLSFO
ISO F-8217:2017 LSMGO TO APPLY
CHARTERERS TO COMPLY WITH FUEL SULPHUR CONTENTS AS PER LATEST REQUIREMENTS OF MARPOL ANNEX VI.
VESSEL ONLY HAVE ONE MGO TANK AND CAN\'T SEPARATE, OWNERS WILL AGREE MIXING LSMGO WITH WHICH ALREADY ON BOARD.
VESSEL HAS LIBERTY TO CONSUME DIESEL OIL BY BOTH M/E AND A/E WHENEVER VESSEL ENCOUNTER HEAVY SEA / SEVERE WEATHER CONDITIONS, MANEUVERING, IN/OUT OF PORTS, NAVIGATING IN SHALLOW / RESTRICTED / BUSY /CONGESTED WATER, CANALS AND RIVERS, STARTING/ STOPPING OF A/E, BURNING OF SLUDGE OIL BY INCINERATOR, DURING LOW LOAD OR UNSTEADY LOADING OPERATION OF A/E.
VSL WILL BURN ADDITIONAL 2MT LSFO PER DAY WHEN HEAVY BALLASTING /DEBALLASTING.
GOOD WEATHER CONDITION IN THS C/P SHALL MEANS ALL WEATHER CONDITONS NOT EXCEEDING WIND FORCE BEAUFORT FOUR, DOUGLAS SEA STATE THREE, NO ADVERSE CURRENT AND NO NEGATIVE INFLUENCE OF SWELL.THE VESSEL\'S PERFORMANCE SHALL ONLY BE ASSESSED ON THE BASIS OF ITS PERFORMANCE IN GOOD WEATHER AS DEFINED IN THIS DESCRIPTION. WHERE CHARTERERS PROVE THAT AN UNDER-PERFORMANCE HAS OCCURRED DURING A GOOD WEATHER PERIOD, ANY CLAIMS ARISING THEREFROM SHALL BE RESTRICTED TO THAT GOOD WEATHER PERIOD ONLY, AND NO CLAIMS WHATSOEVER MAY BE BROUGHT FOR UNDERPERFORMANCE, LOSS OF TIME, OR OVER-CONSUMPTION DURING PERIODS OF TIME THAT ARE NOT GOOD WEATHER PERIODS.
